title: Eigenverft module package definition (schemaVersion 1.2)
$comment: Design rationale: schemaVersion 1.2 separates stable update lanes (packageTargets), concrete version facts (versionCatalog), detection facts (discovery/stateDiscovery), upstreams, and lifecycle behavior (packageOperations). Maintainers should be able to ship a normal update by adding one versionCatalog entry plus per-target hashes; unusual vendor changes can override artifact fields per target.
description: A package definition for the Eigenverft module package installer engine. The runtime builds an effective assigned package from packageTarget + selected version + selected artifact + packageOperations.

$schema.description: Optional editor hint pointing to this JSON schema. It is not used as the runtime version gate.

schemaVersion.description: The package-definition wire format version. Runtime support is intentionally strict; schemaVersion 1.2 definitions do not use 1.1 release/default blocks.

id.description: Stable package definition id used by Invoke-Package and inventory records. Renaming this intentionally creates a new package identity.

display.description: Human-facing package text for output, state views, and future catalog/repository browsing.

dependencies.description: Package definitions that must be assigned before this package can be assigned. Dependencies are package-level because they describe readiness prerequisites, not an individual installer switch.

packageTargets.description: Selectable lanes for platform/channel/architecture. A target answers: which machine shape and update lane can use which catalog artifact?

versionCatalog.description: Concrete package versions and artifact facts. Normal maintainer updates should mostly add one entry here with hashes and target artifacts.

discovery.description: Neutral facts about files, commands, apps, registry probes, and state checks exposed by an installed package. Assignment, validation, shims, and future removal all reuse this data.

stateDiscovery.description: Rules for finding an already-installed package on the machine before deciding whether to reuse, adopt, ignore, repair, or install.

upstreamSources.description: Definition-local upstream endpoints referenced by artifacts. The artifact chooses the source; this block stores the reusable endpoint details.

packageOperations.description: Lifecycle behavior for assigning or removing the selected package. Version facts stay in versionCatalog; operation mechanics stay here.

identifier.description: Portable id segment. Keep ids stable and avoid spaces so paths, inventory keys, and repository references stay predictable.

display.description: Localized display container. Only default text is modeled in 1.2; the shape leaves room for later localization.

displayEntry.description: Default display metadata for humans. These fields do not control source selection or install behavior.

dependency.description: A package-level dependency reference. Repository id is explicit so future repository sources do not silently inherit the wrong package universe.

packageTarget.$comment: Targets are intentionally separate from versions. Maintainers can add a new version without duplicating OS/CPU/channel rules, and can add a new platform target without rewriting old version metadata.
packageTarget.description: A selectable target lane such as stable win-x64, stable arm64, or a model quantization flavor.

id.description: Target id used as the key inside versionCatalog[].artifactsByTarget.

channel.description: Update lane such as stable. This replaces the older releaseTrack wording.

platformTarget.description: Package-facing platform or flavor label, for example win-x64, windows_amd64, q8-0, or 64-bit. This replaces the older flavor wording.

constraints.description: Machine constraints used to decide whether this target is selectable.

versionSelection.description: How the runtime selects a version from versionCatalog for this target.

artifactDefaults.description: Default artifact source/file naming for this target. A versionCatalog artifact can override these when a vendor changes layout.

constraints.description: Platform selection constraints. These are selection filters, not user-facing compatibility warnings.

versionSelection.$comment: latestByVersion sorts the versionCatalog entries and selects the newest matching entry. It is not a network latest lookup.
versionSelection.description: Target-local version choice policy. 1.2 supports deterministic catalog selection only.

artifactDefaults.description: Target-level defaults for artifacts so version entries stay short when source layout is stable across updates.

versionCatalogEntry.$comment: Version entries are the maintainer update surface. Keep source mechanics reusable in packageTargets/upstreamSources unless a specific version truly differs.
versionCatalogEntry.description: One concrete package version and the artifact facts available for one or more packageTargets.

version.description: Package version used for selection, paths, validation tokens, and inventory. It may be semver, a vendor build number, or another stable version string.

releaseTag.description: Optional upstream release tag. Required by runtime validation when a selected artifact uses a githubRelease source.

channels.description: Channels this version participates in, such as stable. A target only considers catalog entries containing its channel.

artifactsByTarget.description: Artifact facts keyed by packageTargets[].id. Keys must match target ids so source selection stays explicit.

artifactForTarget.description: Concrete file/source/hash facts for a version on a specific target. Values here override target artifactDefaults.

releaseId.description: Stable selected-release id written into results and inventory. Use a human-readable id that identifies version, channel, and target.

fileName.description: Expected package file or asset name. For GitHub release artifacts this is the exact asset selector.

sourcePath.description: Optional source-relative path. Supports tokens such as {version}, {releaseTag}, {channel}, and {platformTarget}.

artifactSources.description: Optional source candidates for this version/target. Use this when a specific version changes acquisition behavior.

contentHash.description: Trust boundary for downloaded or depot package files. A valid hash is the acceptance signal, not upstream metadata alone.

publisherSignature.description: Publisher signature requirement for executable package files when fixed hashes are unsuitable, for example evergreen vendor installers.

artifactSources.description: Ordered acquisition candidates. Lower searchOrder is tried first; fallback behavior is controlled by global config.

artifactSource.$comment: packageDepot is durable reuse, download/filesystem are acquisition sources. Raw package files are staged before validation and install.

oneOf.description: Acquire from configured package depots using the package depot relative layout.

kind.description: Search readable package depots for the package file.

verification.description: Verification expectation for this source candidate.

oneOf.description: Acquire from an upstream source defined in upstreamSources.

kind.description: Download from a definition-local upstream source.

sourcePath.description: Optional source-relative path for this candidate. If omitted, artifact sourcePath may supply it.

verification.description: Verification expectation for this source candidate.

oneOf.description: Acquire from a concrete filesystem path or filesystem source.

kind.description: Copy a raw package file from the filesystem.

sourcePath.description: Filesystem path or source-relative file path.

verification.description: Verification expectation for this source candidate.
How strongly this source candidate must be verified. The actual hash/signature rules live on the artifact.

upstreamSource.$comment: Upstream sources are intentionally separate from artifacts so version entries can stay small and source types can be extended later.

oneOf.description: Plain HTTP(S) source. The runtime combines baseUri with sourcePath/package file data.

kind.description: Use direct URL construction from baseUri plus artifact/candidate path.

baseUri.description: Base URI. Keep version-specific path segments in sourcePath when possible so future version updates stay localized.

oneOf.description: GitHub REST release lookup by owner, repository, and selected version releaseTag.

kind.description: Resolve metadata through the GitHub releases/tags API.

discovery.$comment: discovery replaces providedTools plus validation-only entries. The same facts can expose shims, validate assignment, and later verify removal.
discovery.description: Install-state facts relative to the selected install directory. Keep this neutral: it describes what exists, not which operation uses it.

files.description: Files expected relative to the install directory. Used when installedStateCheck.require.files is true.

directories.description: Directories expected relative to the install directory.

commands.description: Command entry points provided by the package. Exposed commands are used for shim generation and dependency command lookup.

apps.description: Application entry points provided by the package. These are not necessarily added to PATH.

metadataFiles.description: Structured metadata file probes used during validation.

signatures.description: Authenticode signature probes for installed files.

registry.description: Registry probes used to validate machine prerequisites or installer state.

discoveredCommand.description: A command executable or command shim target exposed by the installed package.

name.description: Command name as users or dependent packages reference it, for example git, npm, or codex.

relativePath.description: Path to the command relative to the install directory.

required.description: Whether validation should consider this entry mandatory when command checks are enabled.

exposed.description: Whether this command is exposed for shims and dependency command lookup.

stateChecks.description: Command-output checks run against this entry point when installed-state validation requires commands.

discoveredApp.description: A GUI or app executable exposed by the installed package.

commandStateCheck.description: Validation rule for a discovered command. Output can be matched and compared against the selected version through tokens.

metadataFileProbe.description: Validation rule for reading a value from an installed metadata file.

signatureProbe.description: Validation rule for an installed file's Authenticode signature.

fileDetailsProbe.description: Validation rule for Windows file details such as product name or file version.

registryProbe.description: Validation rule for registry-based state, most often machine-wide prerequisites.

stateDiscovery.description: Machine-state discovery groups. In 1.2 only installed state is modeled; future states can be added without changing discovery facts.

installedStateDiscovery.description: How the runtime searches for an already-installed copy before assignment.

enableDetection.description: When false, skip external/current install discovery and only use Package inventory/target paths.

searchLocations.description: Candidate sources for locating installed software, such as command lookup, direct path, directory, or uninstall registry key.

installRootRules.description: Rules that turn a discovered executable/file path into the package install root.

installSearchLocation.description: One place to search for an installed package candidate.

installRootRule.description: Maps a discovered path to an install root, for example code.cmd under bin maps one directory up.

packageOperations.$comment: Operations intentionally reference discovery instead of duplicating file/command facts. Assigned is implemented today; removed is modeled for the desired-state pipeline but execution can be deferred per package.

operationsShared.$comment: Shared operation settings intentionally stay outside assigned/removed actions when they describe package-wide policy rather than one specific process step.
operationsShared.description: Operation settings shared by desired states, especially assignment ownership policy and compatibility gates.

ownershipPolicy.description: How Assigned handles existing installs, adopted installs, and Package-owned inventory.

compatibility.description: Compatibility checks evaluated before Assigned work. Removal is intentionally not blocked by compatibility.

assignedOperation.description: How DesiredState=Assigned makes the selected package ready on the machine.

assignedCommon.description: Common optional assignment controls embedded into each assigned operation kind.

installedStateCheck.description: How to decide whether the desired package state is already present after install or reuse.

versionUpdatePolicy.description: How an already assigned package behaves when the selected catalog version is unchanged or newer.

assignedExpandArchive.description: Assign by expanding an archive into an owned install directory.

kind.description: Uses the generic archive expansion flow.

installDirectory.description: Install directory template relative to the configured installed root unless absolute. Supports package/version/target tokens.

expandedRoot.description: Archive root selection, such as auto or a concrete subdirectory like tools.

createDirectories.description: Directories to create after extraction, relative to the install directory.

installedStateCheck.description: Discovery-backed state check after archive expansion.

versionUpdatePolicy.description: Archive replacement/reuse behavior when assignment is repeated.

assignedNpmGlobalPackage.description: Assign by running npm install -g into a staged prefix and promoting the result into the package install directory.

kind.description: Uses npm as an installer backend for a globally installed CLI package under a Package-owned prefix.

installerCommand.description: Command name resolved from ready package dependencies, for example npm. The runtime does not fall back to system PATH.

packageSpec.description: npm package spec. Use {version} to keep the package version centralized in versionCatalog.

installDirectory.description: Final Package-owned prefix directory that receives the promoted npm global install.

pathRegistration.description: Usually shim-based for CLI packages so only the package shim root needs to be on PATH.

installedStateCheck.description: Discovery-backed state check for generated npm command shims.

versionUpdatePolicy.description: Npm package replacement/reuse behavior when assignment is repeated.

assignedPlacePackageFile.description: Assign by placing a single package file into an install directory, useful for resources such as model files.

kind.description: Copies the verified artifact into the install directory without expanding or executing it.

installDirectory.description: Directory that owns the placed package file, commonly a model/resource folder.

targetRelativePath.description: Relative file path under installDirectory where the prepared artifact is placed.

installedStateCheck.description: Discovery-backed state check for the placed file.

versionUpdatePolicy.description: Resource replacement/reuse behavior when assignment is repeated.

assignedNsisInstaller.description: Assign by running an NSIS installer from the package install stage.

kind.description: Runs a Nullsoft Scriptable Install System installer with NSIS-specific target-directory handling.

installDirectory.description: Target install directory passed to the NSIS installer when targetDirectoryArgument is enabled.

installerKind.description: Human-readable installer family metadata for output and diagnostics.

uiMode.description: Expected UI behavior such as silent or passive. Arguments still remain explicit in commandArguments.

elevation.description: Whether the process should run without elevation, require elevation, or auto-elevate when needed.

timeoutSec.description: Maximum installer runtime in seconds before the operation fails.

commandArguments.description: Installer arguments owned by the package definition. Package-specific switches belong here, not in engine code.

targetDirectoryArgument.description: NSIS target-directory argument configuration. The runtime appends this last because NSIS requires /D=... to be final.

successExitCodes.description: Exit codes treated as successful completion.

restartExitCodes.description: Exit codes treated as successful completion with restart required.

pathRegistration.description: PATH/shim registration after the installer has completed and validation has passed.

installedStateCheck.description: Discovery-backed state check after installer execution.

versionUpdatePolicy.description: Installer replacement/reuse behavior when assignment is repeated.

assignedRunInstaller.description: Assign by running a generic installer process, commonly for machine prerequisites such as Visual C++ Redistributable.

kind.description: Runs a generic installer process without installer-family-specific argument shaping.

targetKind.description: Whether the installer creates a directory-target package or applies a machine-wide prerequisite.

installerKind.description: Installer family metadata, for example burn, msi, inno, or vendor-specific labels.

uiMode.description: Expected UI mode used for diagnostics. The runtime does not infer switches from this field.

elevation.description: Whether the installer must elevate, may auto-elevate, or must stay unelevated.

timeoutSec.description: Maximum installer runtime in seconds before the operation fails.

logRelativePath.description: Optional log path relative to the package log root. Tokens such as {timestamp} may be used by runtime code.

commandArguments.description: Literal ordered installer arguments. Package definitions own switches because installer families vary.

successExitCodes.description: Exit codes treated as successful completion.

restartExitCodes.description: Exit codes treated as successful completion with restart required.

pathRegistration.description: PATH/shim registration after installer completion. Machine prerequisites usually use mode none.

installedStateCheck.description: Discovery-backed state check after installer execution.

versionUpdatePolicy.description: Generic installer replacement/reuse behavior when assignment is repeated.

installedStateCheck.$comment: Installed-state checks intentionally point back to discovery so validation, adoption, update checks, and removal verification do not duplicate file/command/app knowledge.
installedStateCheck.description: Defines which discovery groups must pass for the selected package to be considered installed or ready.

use.description: Use the package discovery section as the source of truth for state checks.

expectedVersion.description: Expected version expression, usually {version}, compared by discovery state checks that expose versions.

require.description: Discovery groups that are mandatory for this state check.

versionUpdatePolicy.$comment: Version update policy is intentionally separated from catalog selection: selection chooses the desired version, this block says how to handle an already assigned different version.
versionUpdatePolicy.description: Assignment behavior when inventory already contains this package slot.

whenAssigned.description: Compare the current inventory version with the version selected from versionCatalog.

onSameSelectedVersion.description: Reuse a healthy install or repair it when the same selected version is already tracked.

onNewSelectedVersion.description: Behavior when the selected catalog version differs from the tracked inventory version.

removedOperation.$comment: Removal is modeled but intentionally smaller than assignment in v1. The operation removes package-owned state; cleanup tells the runtime which side effects to remove afterward.
removedOperation.description: How DesiredState=Removed handles a package that is tracked in inventory.

operation.description: The actual removal mechanism, such as deleting the install directory.

cleanup.description: Which package side effects are cleaned after removal succeeds.

oneOf.description: Remove a Package-owned directory tree. Suitable for archive, npm-prefix, and placed-file packages.
Removal is explicitly unsupported for this package.

verifyAbsent.description: Discovery requirements used to verify the package is absent after removal.

use.description: Use the same discovery model that assignment uses for presence checks.

require.description: Discovery groups that must be absent or no longer accepted after removal.

removeCleanup.description: Side-effect cleanup toggles applied after removal succeeds.

shims.description: Remove generated command shims owned by this package.

path.description: Remove PATH entries owned by this package.

discoveryRequirementFlags.$comment: Flags let each operation reuse discovery but decide which discovery groups matter. For example a resource model may require files but no commands.
discoveryRequirementFlags.description: Boolean switches selecting which discovery groups are required by a state check.

pathRegistration.$comment: PATH registration is separate from install kind so package operations can switch between direct PATH and shims without rewriting installer semantics.
pathRegistration.description: Controls whether the package modifies PATH and which discovered path is registered.

oneOf.description: Register a user or machine PATH entry from a discovered source.

source.description: Discovery-backed source used to compute the registered path or shim root.

pathRegistrationSource.description: Source selector for direct PATH registration or shim generation.

oneOf.description: Generate shims from exposed discovery.commands and register the shared shim root.

kind.description: Use generated command shims instead of placing install directories directly on PATH.

use.description: Generate shims from commands marked exposed=true in discovery.commands.

oneOf.description: Register a path derived from one named command, app, or install-relative directory.

kind.description: How value should be resolved into a path.

value.description: Command/app name or install-relative directory value, depending on kind.

ownershipPolicy.$comment: Ownership policy is assignment policy, not discovery. Discovery finds candidates; this policy decides whether Package may adopt, replace, or require its own inventory.
ownershipPolicy.description: Rules for accepting, adopting, or requiring Package-owned installs during DesiredState=Assigned.

allowAdoptExternal.description: Allow an existing non-Package install to satisfy assignment and be tracked as adopted.

upgradeAdoptedInstall.description: Allow Package to replace an adopted external install when a different selected version is required.

requirePackageOwnership.description: Require Package-owned inventory for the package to be considered ready.

compatibility.$comment: Compatibility checks are gates/advisories for assignment, not proof of installation. Use discovery for installed state.
compatibility.description: Environment checks that decide whether assignment should continue, warn, or fail.

checks.description: Compatibility checks evaluated before assignment work starts.

compatibilityCheck.description: One compatibility check. onFail defaults in runtime behavior when omitted.

kind.description: Compare the current OS version against value.

onFail.description: Whether a rejected check blocks assignment or only warns.

oneOf.description: Memory/resource compatibility check measured in GiB.

kind.description: Memory source to evaluate. physicalOrVideoMemoryGiB passes when either physical RAM or VRAM satisfies the threshold.

onFail.description: Whether a rejected check blocks assignment or only warns.
Compatibility failure behavior. fail blocks assignment; warn records and reports the issue but continues.

contentHash.$comment: Content hash is the deterministic trust boundary for fixed package files. Publisher signatures can add provenance checks but do not replace the hash for pinned artifacts.
contentHash.description: Cryptographic content hash for a package artifact.

algorithm.description: Hash algorithm. Only sha256 is supported in schema 1.2.

value.description: Expected lowercase or uppercase SHA-256 hex digest.

publisherSignature.$comment: Publisher signature is currently modeled for Windows Authenticode because installer EXEs may be vendor-signed while their download URL is not content-addressed.
publisherSignature.description: Publisher signature requirement for executable package artifacts.

requireValid.description: Require the signature chain/status to be valid.

subjectContains.description: Substring expected in the signing certificate subject.

elevationMode.description: Process elevation behavior for installer operations.
